header.subtitle: Labour vulnerability to AI · Spain 2026 · 502 CNO-11 occupations · LFS Q4 2025 · Census 2021
header.title: Job Vulnerability to Artificial Intelligence in Spain
header.description: Analysis of 502 occupations in the Spanish labour market by their degree of AI vulnerability. Data INE/LFS Q4 2025 · Census 2021 weights · Scores calibrated with 5 Spain-specific structural factors (DESI 2023, labour protection, EU AI Act Annex III, AESIA).

stats.employmentMicro: Modelled occupational estimate, not directly observed at 4-digit level.
stats.avgVulnMicro: Interpretive estimate, not a precise measurement.
stats.wageSubtitle: Theoretical indicator — not euros at risk

disclaimer.title: ⚠ Note on employment data:
disclaimer.text: 4-digit CNO figures are estimates (4-layer cascade: LFS 1-digit → LFS 2-digit → Census 3-digit → SEPE 4-digit). These are not individually observed occupation-level figures.
disclaimer.italic: 4-layer cascade estimate — not observed at 4-digit level.

scatter.bubbleNote: Bubble size represents the occupation's share of total employment.

interpretationStrip.theoreticalTooltip: The score measures the theoretical automation potential of tasks, not the effective displacement of workers. Empirical evidence shows real adoption is 3-5× below theoretical exposure.
interpretationStrip.employmentTooltip: 4-digit CNO employment figures are proportional estimates via LFS→Census→SEPE cascade, not directly observed per occupation.
interpretationStrip.euAiAct: EU AI Act = regulatory context, not labour risk
interpretationStrip.euAiActTooltip: The EU AI Act classification refers to AI system deployment in that professional context (Regulation 2024/1689, Annex III). It does not equate to labour displacement risk.

howToRead.title: How to read this map
howToRead.p1: Each rectangle is an occupation (CNO-11 at 4-digit level). Colour indicates AI vulnerability level (blue = low, red = high). Size reflects employment volume.
howToRead.p2: Scores (0-10) are theoretical AI exposure estimates, calibrated with 5 Spain-specific structural factors. They are not displacement predictions.
howToRead.p3: The ≥7 threshold for 'high vulnerability' is indicative. Sensitivity band is 11.7%–15.2% under ±20% variations in calibration factors.
howToRead.p4: 4-digit employment figures are estimates (4-layer cascade). 5 occupations in CNO group 59 (police, civil guard, prisons) use official administrative data.
howToRead.p5: EU AI Act classifies AI systems by use case (Annex III), not occupations. 'High risk' means AI systems in that context are subject to strict requirements, not that the job is at risk.
howToRead.p6: For labour policy or career decisions, consult the full methodology (57 technical notes) available on Zenodo.

detail.wageSubtitle: Theoretical indicator — not euros at risk
detail.weightedIndex: Weighted index, not wage bill at risk
detail.regulatoryExplainer: Regulatory proxy for use-case context, not a legal ruling.
detail.confidenceB: B — SEPE synthetic estimate (dependent on proportional weights)
detail.reliabilityText: 100% coverage (502/502 occupations). Rescaled: r = 0.953 (κ<sub>w</sub> = 0.667). Informative: r = 0.802. D/C/F/R available for all occupations.
detail.shareText: {{name}} (CNO {{cno}}) · AI Vulnerability: {{score}}/10 · {{employees}} employees · Average salary: {{salary}} · Theoretical concentration index: {{index}} · Impact type: {{impact}} · EU AI Act: {{risk}}

tooltip.clickToInspect: Click to inspect {{count}} occupations in this sector

sensitivity.title: Sensitivity analysis: ±20% on calibration factors
sensitivity.description: The 5 calibration factors are expert judgement without empirical back-testing. This table shows how key figures change when each factor is shifted ±20%. The multiplicative formula compounds the uncertainty.
sensitivity.note: Note: "High vulnerability" figures are not linearly recalculated because they depend on individual thresholds per occupation. The salary concentration index scales proportionally.

methodology.title: Methodology:
methodology.text1: 502 CNO-11 occupations (INE/SEPE). Employment: LFS Q4 2025 microdata (22.73M employed), distributed via 4-layer cascade (LFS 1-digit → LFS 2-digit → Census 2021 → SEPE 4-digit). 5 occupations in CNO group 59 use official administrative data (MIR Yearbook, AAPP Bulletin, INAP, Prison Academy).
methodology.text2: Salaries: Wage Structure Survey 2023 (INE), adjusted with INE education premia and international proxies (INSEE France, INE-PT Portugal). 128 unique values.
methodology.text3: AI vulnerability scores (0-10) calibrated with 5 Spain-specific structural factors: (1) DESI 2023 digitalisation index by sector (69.8 aggregate points, 3rd EU — EC no longer publishes composite DESI post-2023), (2) services sector weight, (3) labour protection (Workers' Statute), (4) AI use-case classification per EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689, Annex III — classifies AI systems by use case, not occupations), (5) AESIA supervision (Royal Decree 729/2023).
methodology.wageIndexText: Calculated as Employment × Salary × (Score/10). A weighted index reflecting the concentration of wage bill in high-vulnerability occupations. It does not represent wage bill "at risk" or "threatened" — a high score indicates theoretical vulnerability, not displacement.
methodology.employmentText: 4-digit CNO figures are estimates (4-layer cascade: LFS 1-digit → LFS 2-digit with 62 subgroups → Census 3-digit → SEPE 4-digit). 499 unique values. 5 occupations (CNO 5910, 5921, 5922, 5923, 5991) use official administrative data (A+/A confidence). The LFS includes ~3.3M self-employed; the Wage Structure Survey excludes them by design.
methodology.internationalText: The OECD estimates 28% of jobs at risk of automation in Spain, but that figure includes all technologies (robotics, traditional software, AI). The OECD AI-specific figure for Spain is 5.9% high risk (traditional automation) or 27.4% exposure to generative AI (different construct). Our 12.1% measures theoretical AI vulnerability calibrated with Spain-specific structural factors (47 occupations, 22.73M workers).
methodology.llmText: Scores generated with Gemini 2.5 Pro (T=0.2) and validated with GPT-4o (100 holistic) + GPT 5.4 and Gemini 3 (D/C/F/R sub-components). 100% coverage (502/502 occupations): 177 rescaled (r = 0.953) + 325 informative (r = 0.802). κ<sub>w</sub> = 0.667 (substantial agreement, Landis-Koch). 158 occupations with divergence >2.0 points documented in notes [21] and [47]. Prompts, scripts and raw data published on Zenodo (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19165098).
methodology.noteText: Scores are theoretical vulnerability estimates, not predictions of job displacement. Empirical evidence (Anthropic, February 2026) shows theoretical vulnerability can differ significantly from actual adoption (94% theoretical vs 33% observed in computing/mathematical occupations). Apply a 70-80% discount to estimate current impact. Full methodology with 57 technical notes available on Zenodo.

methodologyPanel.title: Methodology & limitations
methodologyPanel.whatText: Theoretical AI automation vulnerability (0-10) for 502 CNO-11 occupations. Measures task automation potential, not effective worker displacement.
methodologyPanel.whatNotText: Does not measure: (1) actual probability of job loss, (2) adoption speed, (3) net impact (destruction minus creation), (4) retraining effects, (5) inequalities by age/gender/region.
methodologyPanel.employmentText: 4-layer cascade estimate. 499 unique values. 5 public security occupations use official administrative data. Estimated margin of error ±15% for Tier B occupations.
methodologyPanel.salaryText: WSS 2023 (INE). 128 unique values (INE publishes at 2-digit CNO). Validated MAPE: 4.96%. Temporal deflator not applied (CPI +10% 2022→2025) to maintain MAPE validation coherence.
methodologyPanel.scoresText: Generated with LLMs, 100% inter-model validated. 158 occupations with divergence >2 points between holistic and sub-component scoring — documented in notes [21] and [47].
methodologyPanel.euAiActTitle: EU AI Act in this context
methodologyPanel.euAiActText: The EU AI Act classification (Regulation 2024/1689, Annex III) classifies AI systems by use case, not occupations. 'High risk' means AI system deployment in that context requires strict compliance. It does not equate to labour risk or automation probability.
methodologyPanel.sourcesText: INE/LFS Q4 2025, Census 2021, SEPE contracts 2024, WSS 2023, MIR Yearbook 2023, AAPP Bulletin 2024, INAP, Prison Academy/SGIP. Prompts, scripts and raw data: Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19165098.

footer.credit: Methodology v30 · 57 technical notes · Inter-model validation: κw = 0.667 · © 2026 A. de Nicolás, M. Sureda
